*Generated by Claude on May 24, 2026.*

An interactive notebook walking through the Hough Transform and its continuous limit, the Radon Transform — from point↔line duality in Cartesian space, through polar parameterization and accumulator voting, up to the sinogram and filtered backprojection used in CT scanning.

<iframe id="radon-frame" src="/radon/Hough%20to%20Radon.html" width="100%" frameborder="0" scrolling="no" style="border: none; display: block; width: 100%; overflow: hidden;" title="Hough to Radon interactive notebook"></iframe>
<script>
  var frame = document.getElementById('radon-frame');
  function resize() {
    try {
      var h = frame.contentDocument.documentElement.scrollHeight;
      if (h > 100) frame.style.height = h + 'px';
    } catch(e) {}
  }
  frame.addEventListener('load', function() {
    resize();
    // React + Babel renders async — keep syncing until height stabilises
    if (window.ResizeObserver) {
      try {
        var ro = new ResizeObserver(resize);
        ro.observe(frame.contentDocument.body);
      } catch(e) {}
    }
    // Fallback checkpoints for browsers without ResizeObserver
    [200, 500, 1000, 2000, 4000].forEach(function(t) { setTimeout(resize, t); });
  });
</script>
